text: oh so THATS why my project files look like a dumpster fire after a week
it is all fun and games until you realize you named your final video file 'final_v2_real_final_fixed.mp4'
wait is this why some people use those weird numbering systems like 010, 020?
exactly. if you dont leave gaps in your numbering you are just asking for pain when you have to insert a new edit later.
the real pro move is using ISO 8601 dates at the start of every file name.
ok but like... does anyone actually organize their assets before starting the edit? i always just dump everything into one folder and pray.
that works until you have to relink media on a different machine and the software cant find anything.
this. once you have had to manually hunt for 50 missing files, you learn real quick to use a proper directory structure.
the chaos method is a valid workflow if your project is just a 30 second tiktok
i still dont get why people use nested sequences for simple cuts. seems like overkill.
